frameworks.name: structure learning
frameworks.one_line: learning relations among objects/categories in a visual scene

frameworks.name: low-dimensional manifold coding
frameworks.one_line: PPC embeds high-dim inputs on a low-dim relational manifold

frameworks.name: deep learning (feedforward vision models)
frameworks.one_line: lacks relational inference of the primate dorsal stream

frameworks.name: embodied/action-grounded cognition
frameworks.one_line: structure learning grounded in reaching and eye fixations

core_question: How does the primate posterior parietal cortex support learning of relational structure among objects and abstract entities?
key_assumptions: PPC encodes spatial and abstract relations on a shared low-dim code
relational inference is central to global scene understanding
key_findings: PPC neural codes project high-dim inputs onto a low-dim manifold
the manifold encodes relative positions of objects and abstract entity relations
current deep vision models fail at relational inferences performed in dorsal stream
interpretations: low-dim PPC code supports generalisation of relational information beyond spatial domains
structure learning is grounded in primate reaching and fixating actions
